title: Doellingeria ledophylla
taxonomy: Doellingeria ledophylla is classified within the family Asteraceae. This classification places it in the subfamily Asteroideae and tribe Senecioneae. Further, it is a member of the genus Doellingeria, which is a group of flowering plants. The genus Doellingeria is often included in the subfamily Cichorioideae. As a result, the classification of Doellingeria ledophylla is complex and involves multiple levels of classification.

parts: Doellingeria ledophylla, also known as ledum or bog elder, is a shrub native to North America. The medicinal parts of Doellingeria ledophylla are primarily its leaves and roots, which have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The roots of the plant are reported to have anti-inflammatory properties, while the leaves have been used to treat conditions such as fever and rheumatism. The plant's medicinal properties are thought to be due to the presence of compounds such as sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Doellingeria ledophylla.
preparations: Doellingeria ledophylla is a species of flowering plant known for its potential medicinal properties, which have led to the development of herbal preparations. intro: Doellingeria leptophylla, also known as the flat-topped wood's aster, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to alleviate various health issues, including inflammation, digestive problems, and respiratory conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Doellingeria leptophylla include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
